description: Controlled test scenarios for evaluating long-term dialogue coherence and memory retention in the baseline Llama 3.1 8B dialogue system. Each scenario introduces specific facts in Session 1, then tests recall in Session 2 (new context window). This measures cross-session memory retention, coherence, and hallucination frequency.

scoring.retention_accuracy: Binary (0 or 1) per fact recalled correctly when probed in Session 2
scoring.dialogue_coherence: Scale 0.0-1.0; scored as proportion of responses that maintain logical consistency with established context
scoring.hallucination_frequency: Proportion of factual responses containing information not in ground truth
scoring.forgotten_information_rate: Proportion of introduced facts not recalled when probed

scenarios.name: Personal Identity and Preferences
scenarios.description: User introduces personal information (name, occupation, hobby) in Session 1. Session 2 tests recall of this information.
session_1_facts: User works as a software engineer

session_1_dialogue.user: Hi, I'm Alex, a software engineer. Can you help me with a coding question?
I've been working on a Python REST API this weekend, though I usually prefer hiking on weekends.
I'm also trying to learn Spanish in my spare time. Any tips?
I'm based in Manchester, so the weather hasn't been great for outdoor activities lately.
Anyway, back to the coding question – how do I handle async tasks in FastAPI?
session_1_dialogue.expected_behavior: answers coding question, may reference user context

session_1_dialogue.user: Great, and what about error handling in FastAPI?
Do you think learning Spanish will help me professionally as a software engineer?
session_1_dialogue.expected_behavior: responds referencing both occupation and language learning

session_1_dialogue.user: How about local tech meetups in Manchester for someone learning new skills?
session_1_dialogue.expected_behavior: responds referencing location and learning context

session_1_dialogue.user: One more Python question – how do I manage database connections with SQLAlchemy?
Thanks for the help today, I'll try applying this on my hike tomorrow if it stops raining!
session_1_dialogue.expected_behavior: friendly close referencing hobby and location context

session_2_probe_questions.user: Hello again. Can you remind me what you know about me from our last chat?
What is my name?
What do I do for work?
What hobby did I mention I enjoy?
What language am I learning?
Where do I live?

scenarios.name: Project Development Tracking
scenarios.description: User discusses a software project over Session 1 with specific technical decisions. Session 2 tests whether the model recalls those decisions.

session_1_dialogue.user: I'm building a health tracking app called HealthTrack. Where should I start with the architecture?
I've decided to use Django REST Framework for the backend. Good choice?
For the database, I'm going with PostgreSQL because of its reliability.
The frontend will be React with TypeScript. Any component library recommendations?
I plan to deploy on AWS EC2. How do I set up the Django server there?
How do I connect Django to PostgreSQL in a production environment?
What authentication approach would you recommend for HealthTrack?
session_1_dialogue.expected_behavior: advice referencing project name and context

session_1_dialogue.user: How do I handle CORS between the React frontend and Django backend?
session_1_dialogue.expected_behavior: continues technical discussion with project context

session_1_dialogue.user: Any tips for health data security with PostgreSQL?
Thanks, I think I have a good plan now for HealthTrack.

session_2_probe_questions.user: Hi, I need to continue working on my app. Do you remember its name?
What backend framework did I choose for my project?
Which database am I using?
What is my frontend stack?
Where am I deploying my app?

scenarios.name: Factual Knowledge with Hallucination Testing
scenarios.description: Tests the model's tendency to hallucinate when asked factual questions, both within and across sessions. Ground truth answers provided for comparison.

ground_truth_facts.question: What year was the Transformer architecture introduced?
What is the context window size of Llama 3.1 8B?
What is the parameter count of Llama 3.1 8B?
What vector similarity search library is commonly used with LLMs?
What is the working memory model proposed by Baddeley?
ground_truth_facts.answer: A multi-component model with central executive, phonological loop, visuospatial sketchpad, and episodic buffer

ground_truth_facts.question: What company developed the Llama 3.1 model?
What is the primary limitation of context-window-based LLM memory?
ground_truth_facts.answer: Information outside the context window becomes inaccessible; no cross-session persistence

session_1_dialogue.user: What year was the Transformer architecture introduced?
How many tokens can Llama 3.1 8B process in its context window?
What does RAG stand for in the context of LLMs?
How many parameters does Llama 3.1 8B have?
What is FAISS and what is it used for?
What is Baddeley's working memory model?
Who developed the Llama 3.1 model?
What is the main limitation of context-window-based memory in LLMs?

session_2_probe_questions.user: In our last session, you answered a question about the Transformer architecture year. What year did you say?
What did we discuss earlier about Llama 3.1's context window?

scenarios.name: Multi-Session Continuity
scenarios.description: User has a multi-topic conversation in Session 1. Session 2 references multiple earlier topics to test broader coherence across sessions.
session_1_facts: User's research topic: neural network pruning
User has completed literature review and baseline implementation

session_1_dialogue.user: I'm doing dissertation research on neural network pruning. Can you explain the main approaches?
My supervisor Dr Smith suggested I look at magnitude-based pruning first.
My deadline is 3rd May 2026, so I need to be efficient with time.
I'm implementing in PyTorch, not TensorFlow. Any pruning libraries I should know about?
I've finished my literature review and the baseline model implementation.
What experiments should I run to evaluate my pruning approach?
How do I measure accuracy loss due to pruning in PyTorch?
Dr Smith wants me to compare structured vs unstructured pruning. What's the difference?
Given my deadline, what milestones should I aim for?
Thanks, I'll discuss this with Dr Smith tomorrow.

session_2_probe_questions.user: Hi, I want to continue our conversation from before. What topic are we working on?
What did I say my deadline was?
Which framework do I prefer for implementation?
Who is my supervisor?
What have I already completed in my dissertation?

scenarios.name: Coherence Under Information Overload
scenarios.description: Tests dialogue coherence when many facts are introduced quickly in Session 1. Measures within-session coherence degradation and cross-session recall.
session_1_facts: User wants to build an e-commerce app
Key features: inventory management, invoicing, multi-currency support

session_1_dialogue.user: I need to plan an e-commerce application for UK small businesses.
We have a budget of £5,000 and a team of 3 developers.
We want to launch by September 2026.
Our stack is Node.js for the backend, MongoDB for data, and Vue.js for the frontend.
The key features are inventory management, invoicing, and multi-currency support.
Given our budget, how should we divide the work among 3 developers?
session_1_dialogue.expected_behavior: advice referencing budget and team size

session_1_dialogue.user: Can Vue.js handle the multi-currency display requirements?
session_1_dialogue.expected_behavior: technical advice referencing frontend and feature

session_1_dialogue.user: How do we handle UK tax compliance in the invoicing module?
session_1_dialogue.expected_behavior: advice referencing UK market and feature

session_1_dialogue.user: What MongoDB schema would you recommend for inventory management?
session_1_dialogue.expected_behavior: technical advice referencing database and feature

session_1_dialogue.user: Does our timeline seem realistic given our constraints?

session_2_probe_questions.user: Hello, continuing from last time. What kind of application are we building?
session_2_probe_questions.expected_answer: e-commerce application for UK small businesses

session_2_probe_questions.user: What is our budget?
When do we want to launch?
What tech stack are we using?
What are the three key features of our app?
